summary: A fortified castle-like structure with four corner towers connected by crenellated walls. The building features a rectangular footprint with prominent corner pillars, dark stone construction, and decorative overhanging eaves on each tower. The central courtyard area is open and accessible, with a dark red/maroon crenellated battlement running along the top of the connecting walls.

shape.overall_form: rectangular fortress with four corner towers

rebuild_hints: Build four corner pillars approximately 3 blocks wide and 9 blocks tall
Connect pillars with 2-block-high walls featuring crenellations (dark wood blocks alternating with gaps)
Add overhanging slab eaves on each tower top, extending 2-3 blocks outward
Place small square window openings (1x1) scattered on tower faces
Use stonebrick for primary construction with brick accents for texture variation
Create foundation layer with stone blocks around perimeter
Ensure open courtyard in center with no roof coverage
Maintain symmetrical placement of all elements
uncertainties: Exact interior floor layout not visible from exterior views
Precise window placement pattern uncertain due to viewing angles
Whether interior has multiple floors or is hollow uncertain
Exact height of crenellations relative to tower height
Specific brick-to-stonebrick ratio in wall construction
